data.title: ImmunoPrecise's LENSai™ flags Anti-Drug-Antibody risk early; study shows up to 40% biologics fail in late development.
data.description: ImmunoPrecise Antibodies Ltd. revealed in a recent press release that their LENSai™ Immunogenicity Screening has shown promising results in predicting Anti-Drug-Antibody (ADA) risk for therapeutic proteins before animal studies or human trials. The study, conducted by the company, indicates that ADA-related failures currently affect around 40% of biologics in late development stages. ImmunoPrecise Antibodies Ltd., listed on NASDAQ as IPA, is a pioneer in Bio-Native AI technology and integrates biological intelligence, offering innovative solutions at the intersection of TechBio.

data.title: Hippocratic AI in Palo Alto gets HITRUST e1 Certification - a gold standard for healthcare cybersecurity.
data.description: In Palo Alto, California, the achievement of HITRUST certification is recognized as a top-tier standard for cybersecurity in the healthcare industry. This certification is highly esteemed and signifies a substantial level of security for healthcare organizations, demonstrating compliance with industry-specific regulations and standards.

data.title: Swoop® MRI system in studies expands dementia care access, innovation for Alzheimer’s treatment.
data.description: In Guilford, Connecticut, recent studies highlight the potential of the Swoop® MRI system in increasing access to dementia care and tracking Alzheimer's treatment. The advanced system offers promising possibilities for improving healthcare services for patients suffering from cognitive disorders. This development underscores the system's significance in reaching a wider population in need of specialized medical attention.

data.title: ACNB Corp. (NASDAQ: ACNB) raises quarterly dividend to $0.34 per share, 6.3% increase from previous quarter.
data.description: ACNB Corporation, operating as a financial holding company for ACNB Bank and ACNB Insurance Services, Inc., revealed that their Board of Directors has sanctioned a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.34 per share for its common stock (NASDAQ: ACNB). The dividend is set to be paid on September 15, 2025, to shareholders on record as of September 1, 2025. This amount marks a 6.3% upsurge from the previous quarter's payout of $0.32 per share earlier in 2025.

data.title: Sun Life to announce Q2 earnings on Aug 7, 2025; webcast on Aug 8 at 10:00 am. (TSX: SLF) (NYSE: SLF)
data.description: On July 24, 2025, Sun Life Financial Inc. (TSX: SLF) (NYSE: SLF) announced that it is set to reveal its second-quarter financial results on August 7, 2025, after the market closes. Interested individuals can tune in to the live webcast of the results on August 8, 2025, starting at 10:00 a.m.

data.title: The Henry Ford hosts National WWII Museum curator for Servicewomen event on July 31, 2025 in Dearborn, MI. Celebrating women in service. #WWII #OurWarToo
data.description: On Thursday, July 31, 2025 in Dearborn, Michigan, The Henry Ford will be hosting an event to honor servicewomen as part of the Our War Too exhibition. This program aims to showcase the often overlooked and varied experiences of women in the military. Featuring personal stories and accounts, this event is a tribute to the significant contributions made by women in service. The exhibition sheds light on the diverse roles played by servicewomen and highlights their courage and dedication. Join us in celebrating the remarkable stories of these women who have served their country.

data.title: iDEAL Semiconductor partners with Mouser for global distribution as they enter mass production of SuperQ power devices. First major MOSFET advance in over 25 years.
data.description: An innovative technology called SuperQ has recently emerged as a significant development in silicon MOSFET design, representing the most notable advancement since the introduction of Superjunction more than 25 years ago. The groundbreaking technology is brought forth by iDEAL Semiconductor, a fabless power semiconductor company located in BETHLEHEM, Pa. This advance is set to revolutionize the field by delivering remarkable efficiencies. The unveiling occurred on July 24, 2025, marking a milestone in semiconductor architecture progress.

data.title: China's recommerce market to reach $40.01 billion by 2025, growing at 11.0% annually. Robust growth with 13.4% CAGR from 2020-2024.
data.description: A report on the China Recommerce Market Intelligence Databook containing over 60 key performance indicators has recently been included in the offerings of ResearchAndMarkets.com. It predicts that the recommerce market in China will see a noteworthy 11.0% annual growth, reaching a total of US$40.01 billion by 2025. Between 2020 and 2024, the market experienced strong expansion, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 13.4%. This positive trend is anticipated to continue in the coming years.

data.title: Insmed stock hits 25-year high with potential approval of second drug; CEO describes it as a "skeleton key."
data.description: Shares of Insmed are experiencing a significant increase in value as the company nears the possible approval of its second medication. CEO Will Lewis has described this new drug as a "skeleton key." The stock is currently trading at its highest level in 25 years, generating interest and optimism among investors about the potential success of the medication.

data.title: Google Cloud's impressive Q2 results, despite increased capital spending, led to a rise in stock price and delivered a message to Amazon and Microsoft.
data.description: In 2025, Google's stock rose despite an increase in capital spending, attributed to the impressive performance of Google's cloud computing sector in Q2. This success was significant enough to catch the attention of Wall Street, overshadowing concerns about higher spending. The positive outcomes from Google Cloud in Q2 sent a strong message to competitors like Amazon and Microsoft, showcasing the company's prowess in this sector. This news was highlighted without mentioning the specific source to avoid direct attribution.
